title: Ah! Did You Ever Hear The Spring
content:
Ah! did you ever hear the Spring
        Calling you through the snow,
    Or hear the little blackbird sing
        Inside its egg - or go
    To that green land where grass begins,
        Each tiny seed, to grow?

    O have you heard what none has heard,
        Or seen what none has seen;
    O have you been to that strange land
        Where no one else has been!
meaning:
This evocative piece by Richard Le Gallienne represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance, inviting the reader to reflect on the deeper themes of human experience and artistic expression.

In the broader context of Richard Le Gallienne's bibliography, these specific lines stand out for their clarity and poignancy. Whether you are searching for inspiration or a moment of quiet contemplation, this work offers a timeless perspective that transcends its original era.

At Linespedia, we have curated this specific poem to ensure it reaches a modern audience. We recommend sharing these lines as a digital poster or using them as a thoughtful caption to connect with others who appreciate the enduring beauty of verse.
